_note: Snapshot of federal officeholders as of the start of the 119th Congress and the second Trump administration. Update this file when cabinet secretaries, Supreme Court justices, or congressional leadership change. Historical predecessors are retained in 'predecessor' fields for context.

president.name: Donald J. Trump
president.selection_detail: Elected November 5, 2024; inaugurated January 20, 2025 as the 47th President of the United States. Previously served as the 45th President (2017–2021).

contact.dc_office: The White House, 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20500 (Oval Office)

top_issues.name: Trade & Tariffs
top_issues.stance: Advocates for protective tariffs and renegotiating trade agreements to prioritize American manufacturing.

top_issues.name: Immigration
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes border security, stricter enforcement, and limits on asylum and legal immigration pathways.

top_issues.name: Energy Independence
top_issues.stance: Promotes domestic oil, gas, and coal production while limiting environmental regulations.

top_issues.name: Government Efficiency
top_issues.stance: Supports reducing federal workforce and eliminating what he views as wasteful bureaucratic agencies.

experience.note: The Apprentice and Celebrity Apprentice host

experience.role: New York City Real Estate Investor

vice_president.name: JD Vance
vice_president.role: Vice President of the United States
vice_president.selection_detail: Elected as Trump's running mate on Nov 5, 2024. Previously a U.S. Senator from Ohio (2023–2025). Serves as President of the Senate ex officio.

contact.dc_office: Eisenhower Executive Office Building, 17th Street & Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20500

top_issues.name: Populism & Working Class
top_issues.stance: Champions working-class concerns and criticizes corporate globalism in favor of nationalist economic policies.

top_issues.name: American Manufacturing
top_issues.stance: Supports reshoring manufacturing and protecting domestic industry from foreign competition.

top_issues.name: Cultural Issues
top_issues.stance: Advocates for traditional values and skepticism of progressive cultural movements.

cabinet.name: Marco Rubio
cabinet.selection_detail: Nominated by President Trump, confirmed by the Senate 99–0 on Jan 20, 2025. Previously a U.S. Senator from Florida (2011–2025).

contact.dc_office: Harry S Truman Building, 2201 C Street NW, Washington, DC 20520

top_issues.name: China Strategy
top_issues.stance: Advocates for confronting Chinese geopolitical influence and economic competition through diplomatic pressure.

top_issues.name: Latin America
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes relations with Caribbean and Central American countries, particularly regarding Venezuela and Cuba.

top_issues.name: Israel & Middle East
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes strong U.S. support for Israel and opposition to Iranian influence in the region.

cabinet.name: Scott Bessent
cabinet.selection_detail: Hedge-fund manager and founder of Key Square Group. Confirmed by the Senate 68–29 on Jan 27, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Main Treasury Building, 1500 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20220

top_issues.name: Fiscal Policy
top_issues.stance: Focuses on reducing government spending while managing debt and maintaining economic growth.

top_issues.name: Currency Markets
top_issues.stance: Works to stabilize the dollar and manage international currency relationships.

top_issues.name: Financial Regulation
top_issues.stance: Supports selective deregulation of financial institutions while maintaining systemic stability.

cabinet.name: Pete Hegseth
cabinet.selection_detail: Former Army National Guard officer and Fox News host. Confirmed by the Senate 51–50 on Jan 24, 2025 with VP Vance casting the tiebreaking vote.

contact.dc_office: The Pentagon, 1400 Defense Pentagon, Washington, DC 20301

top_issues.name: Military Readiness
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes modernizing U.S. military capability and maintaining strategic advantages over near-peer competitors.

top_issues.name: China & Deterrence
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes military preparedness to deter Chinese aggression in the Indo-Pacific region.

top_issues.name: Military Culture
top_issues.stance: Advocates for merit-based advancement and traditional military values in service organization.

cabinet.name: Pam Bondi
cabinet.selection_detail: Former Attorney General of Florida (2011–2019). Confirmed by the Senate 54–46 on Feb 4, 2025 after Trump's initial nominee, Matt Gaetz, withdrew.

contact.dc_office: Robert F. Kennedy Department of Justice Building, 950 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20530

top_issues.name: Law & Order
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes aggressive prosecution of crime and supports law enforcement with expanded investigative powers.

top_issues.name: Election Integrity
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes vigilance against voter fraud and election irregularities in federal elections.

top_issues.name: Constitutional Rights
top_issues.stance: Advocates for protections of Second Amendment rights and First Amendment speech protections.

cabinet.name: Doug Burgum
cabinet.selection_detail: Former Governor of North Dakota (2016–2024). Confirmed by the Senate 79–18 on Jan 30, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Main Interior Building, 1849 C Street NW, Washington, DC 20240

top_issues.name: Energy Extraction
top_issues.stance: Supports increased oil, gas, and mineral leasing on federal lands to generate revenue and energy independence.

top_issues.name: Tribal Relations
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes partnership with tribal nations while balancing resource development and infrastructure needs.

top_issues.name: Western Land Management
top_issues.stance: Advocates for state-level input on federal land management decisions affecting rural communities.

experience.role: Business Executive, Great Plains Software & Microsoft

cabinet.name: Brooke Rollins
cabinet.selection_detail: Previously headed the America First Policy Institute. Confirmed by the Senate 72–28 on Feb 13, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Jamie L. Whitten Building, 1400 Independence Avenue SW, Washington, DC 20250

top_issues.name: Farm Support
top_issues.stance: Advocates for robust commodity support programs and direct assistance to agricultural producers.

top_issues.name: Agricultural Trade
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes fair trade practices and fighting unfair foreign competition for American farmers.

top_issues.name: Rural Development
top_issues.stance: Focuses on strengthening rural infrastructure and supporting small and mid-size farming operations.

cabinet.name: Howard Lutnick
cabinet.selection_detail: Chairman and CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald. Confirmed by the Senate 51–45 on Feb 18, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Herbert C. Hoover Building, 1401 Constitution Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20230

top_issues.name: Trade Policy
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes negotiating favorable trade deals and protecting U.S. manufacturing through strategic tariffs.

top_issues.name: Technology & Innovation
top_issues.stance: Supports semiconductor industry investment and technology leadership against foreign competitors.

top_issues.name: Business Growth
top_issues.stance: Advocates for deregulation and creating a favorable environment for business expansion and job creation.

cabinet.name: Lori Chavez-DeRemer
cabinet.selection_detail: Former U.S. Representative from Oregon (2023–2025). Confirmed by the Senate 67–32 on Mar 10, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Frances Perkins Building, 200 Constitution Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20210

top_issues.name: Job Creation
top_issues.stance: Focuses on removing regulatory barriers to job growth and supporting small business employment expansion.

top_issues.name: Apprenticeships
top_issues.stance: Advocates for expanding registered apprenticeship programs as alternative pathways to careers.

top_issues.name: Wage Growth
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes policies that support rising wages while maintaining business competitiveness.

cabinet.name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
cabinet.role: Secretary of Health and Human Services
cabinet.department: Department of Health and Human Services
cabinet.selection_detail: Former Democrat and 2024 independent presidential candidate. Confirmed by the Senate 52–48 on Feb 13, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Hubert H. Humphrey Building, 200 Independence Avenue SW, Washington, DC 20201

top_issues.name: Health Agencies Reform
top_issues.stance: Advocates for restructuring federal health agencies and reducing what he views as regulatory overreach.

top_issues.name: Chronic Disease
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes environmental and nutritional factors in chronic disease prevention and research.

top_issues.name: Pharmaceutical Industry
top_issues.stance: Questions pharmaceutical industry practices and promotes transparency in drug development and approval.

cabinet.name: Scott Turner
cabinet.role: Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
cabinet.department: Department of Housing and Urban Development
cabinet.selection_detail: Former NFL player and Texas state representative; led Opportunity and Revitalization Council in Trump's first term. Confirmed 55–44 on Feb 5, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Robert C. Weaver Federal Building, 451 7th Street SW, Washington, DC 20410

top_issues.name: Opportunity Zones
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes economic development in disadvantaged communities through market-based revitalization strategies.

top_issues.name: Housing Affordability
top_issues.stance: Focuses on removing regulatory barriers to housing construction and homeownership.

top_issues.name: Urban Renewal
top_issues.stance: Advocates for community-led development and partnerships with local leadership in revitalization efforts.

cabinet.name: Sean Duffy
cabinet.selection_detail: Former U.S. Representative from Wisconsin (2011–2019). Confirmed by the Senate 77–22 on Jan 28, 2025.

contact.dc_office: 1200 New Jersey Avenue SE, Washington, DC 20590

top_issues.name: Infrastructure Investment
top_issues.stance: Advocates for strategic infrastructure spending focused on real-world transportation needs and efficiency.

top_issues.name: Regulatory Reform
top_issues.stance: Pushes for streamlining transportation regulations to reduce barriers to project completion.

top_issues.name: Safety & Standards
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes maintaining transportation safety standards while reducing unnecessary compliance burdens.

cabinet.name: Chris Wright
cabinet.selection_detail: Founder and CEO of Liberty Energy (oilfield services). Confirmed by the Senate 59–38 on Feb 3, 2025.

contact.dc_office: James V. Forrestal Building, 1000 Independence Avenue SW, Washington, DC 20585

top_issues.name: Energy Production
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes maximizing domestic energy production from all sources to ensure American energy independence.

top_issues.name: Oil & Gas Development
top_issues.stance: Advocates for continued expansion of oil and gas development as essential to U.S. energy security.

top_issues.name: Nuclear Energy
top_issues.stance: Supports nuclear power expansion as part of a comprehensive American energy portfolio.

cabinet.name: Linda McMahon
cabinet.selection_detail: Co-founder of WWE; led the Small Business Administration during Trump's first term. Confirmed 51–45 on Mar 3, 2025. Trump signed an executive order in March 2025 directing the department's dismantlement.

contact.dc_office: Lyndon Baines Johnson Department of Education Building, 400 Maryland Avenue SW, Washington, DC 20202

top_issues.name: School Choice
top_issues.stance: Advocates for expanding school choice options including private schools, charter schools, and homeschooling alternatives.

top_issues.name: Parental Rights
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes parental involvement in education decisions and objections to curriculum content.

top_issues.name: Education Standards
top_issues.stance: Supports higher academic standards and accountability in schools, along with reducing federal education bureaucracy.

cabinet.name: Doug Collins
cabinet.selection_detail: Former U.S. Representative from Georgia (2013–2021) and Air Force Reserve chaplain. Confirmed 77–23 on Feb 4, 2025.

contact.dc_office: Veterans Affairs Central Office, 810 Vermont Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20420

top_issues.name: Veteran Benefits
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes improving access to healthcare, benefits, and services for all veterans.

top_issues.name: VA Modernization
top_issues.stance: Focuses on modernizing VA systems and reducing bureaucratic inefficiencies in delivering services.

top_issues.name: Military Community Support
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes support for active duty, reserve, and retired military personnel and their families.

experience.role: Major, U.S. Air Force Reserve Chaplain

cabinet.name: Kristi Noem
cabinet.selection_detail: Former Governor of South Dakota (2019–2025). Confirmed by the Senate 59–34 on Jan 25, 2025.

contact.dc_office: St. Elizabeths West Campus, 2707 Martin Luther King Jr. Avenue SE, Washington, DC 20528

top_issues.name: Border Security
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes strict border enforcement and stopping illegal immigration as national security priorities.

top_issues.name: Immigration Enforcement
top_issues.stance: Advocates for aggressive interior enforcement and cooperation with local law enforcement on immigration.

top_issues.name: Cybersecurity
top_issues.stance: Supports protecting critical infrastructure and federal systems from cyber threats and intrusions.

supreme_court.body_name: Supreme Court of the United States
supreme_court._note: Justices are nominated by the President, confirmed by the Senate, and hold their office during good behavior (effectively for life).

members.name: John G. Roberts Jr.
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President George W. Bush to the Chief Justice seat after William Rehnquist's death. Confirmed by the Senate 78–22 on Sep 29, 2005.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Constitutional Originalism
top_issues.stance: Interprets the Constitution according to its original public meaning while considering historical context.

top_issues.name: Judicial Restraint
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes deference to elected branches and narrowly tailored judicial decisions with limited scope.

top_issues.name: Federalism
top_issues.stance: Protects state authority and limits federal power expansion in constitutional interpretation.

experience.role: Chief Justice of the United States
Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (D.C. Circuit)
Deputy Solicitor General, U.S. Department of Justice

members.name: Clarence Thomas
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President George H.W. Bush to the seat vacated by Thurgood Marshall. Confirmed 52–48 on Oct 15, 1991.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Originalism & Textualism
top_issues.stance: Strictly interprets constitutional text according to its original public meaning at the time of adoption.

top_issues.name: Individual Liberty
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes constitutional protections for individual freedoms and limits on government regulation.

top_issues.name: Property Rights
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes strong protection for private property and constitutional takings limitations.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (D.C. Circuit)

members.name: Samuel A. Alito Jr.
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President George W. Bush to the seat vacated by Sandra Day O'Connor. Confirmed 58–42 on Jan 31, 2006.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Originalism
top_issues.stance: Interprets the Constitution based on its original public meaning rather than evolving standards.

top_issues.name: Religious Liberty
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes protections for religious expression and conscience rights under the First Amendment.

top_issues.name: Constitutional Separation of Powers
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes strict adherence to constitutional boundaries between branches and levels of government.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (Third Circuit)
Deputy Solicitor General, U.S. Department of Justice
Attorney, U.S. Department of Justice Civil Division

members.name: Sonia Sotomayor
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Obama to the seat vacated by David Souter. First Hispanic Justice. Confirmed 68–31 on Aug 6, 2009.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Criminal Justice
top_issues.stance: Advocates for defendant protections and scrutinizes government power in criminal procedure and Fourth Amendment cases.

top_issues.name: Civil Rights
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes constitutional protections against discrimination and safeguarding vulnerable populations.

top_issues.name: Living Constitution
top_issues.stance: Interprets constitutional provisions as evolving to meet contemporary values and social circumstances.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (Second Circuit)
Judge, New York State Supreme Court
Assistant District Attorney, New York County

members.name: Elena Kagan
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Obama to the seat vacated by John Paul Stevens. Former U.S. Solicitor General. Confirmed 63–37 on Aug 5, 2010.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: First Amendment & Speech
top_issues.stance: Advocates expansively for First Amendment protections and skepticism of government speech restrictions.

top_issues.name: Administrative Law
top_issues.stance: Considers statutory purpose and practical consequences in interpreting congressional intent and regulations.

top_issues.name: Equal Protection
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes dignity and equal treatment in constitutional interpretation of rights and classifications.

experience.role: Solicitor General of the United States
Professor of Law, Harvard Law School

members.name: Neil M. Gorsuch
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Trump to the seat held open after Antonin Scalia's death in 2016. Confirmed 54–45 on Apr 7, 2017.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Textualism
top_issues.stance: Focuses on the plain language and text of statutes and constitutional provisions without resort to legislative history.

top_issues.name: Individual Rights
top_issues.stance: Protects religious liberty, Native American rights, and individual freedoms against government overreach.

top_issues.name: Separation of Powers
top_issues.stance: Upholds constitutional limits on executive and legislative authority and protects judicial independence.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (Tenth Circuit)
Private Practice Attorney, Boies Schiller Flexner

members.name: Brett M. Kavanaugh
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Trump to the seat vacated by Anthony Kennedy. Confirmed 50–48 on Oct 6, 2018.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Originalism
top_issues.stance: Interprets the Constitution and statutes based on their original meaning and text.

top_issues.name: Judicial Restraint
top_issues.stance: Favors narrowly tailored decisions and deference to political branches on policy matters.

top_issues.name: Administrative Law
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes proper statutory interpretation and limitations on executive agency power and discretion.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (D.C. Circuit)

members.name: Amy Coney Barrett
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Trump to the seat vacated by Ruth Bader Ginsburg's death. Confirmed 52–48 on Oct 26, 2020.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Originalism
top_issues.stance: Interprets constitutional and statutory text according to its original public meaning at the time of adoption.

top_issues.name: Religious Liberty
top_issues.stance: Provides robust protection for religious practice and conscience objections under constitutional law.

top_issues.name: Constitutional Structure
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes proper separation of powers and enumerated powers limitations in constitutional interpretation.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (Seventh Circuit)
Professor of Law, University of Notre Dame

members.name: Ketanji Brown Jackson
members.selection_detail: Nominated by President Biden to the seat vacated by Stephen Breyer's retirement. First Black woman Justice. Confirmed 53–47 on Apr 7, 2022.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of the United States, 1 First Street NE, Washington, DC 20543

top_issues.name: Criminal Justice
top_issues.stance: Scrutinizes government power in criminal cases and advocates for defendant protections and fairness.

top_issues.name: Civil Rights & Equity
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes constitutional guarantees of equal protection and addresses systemic discrimination.

top_issues.name: Public Interest Law
top_issues.stance: Draws on public defender experience to shape jurisprudence around access to justice and representation.

experience.role: Judge, U.S. Court of Appeals (D.C. Circuit)
Public Defender, Federal Public Defender's Office (D.C.)

congress._note: Leadership selected at the start of the 119th Congress on Jan 3, 2025. Party breakdown reflects seats at the opening of the Congress and may shift as vacancies, resignations, or party-switches occur.

party_breakdown._note: The two Independents (Sanders-VT, King-ME) caucus with Democrats, giving Republicans a 53–47 working majority.

leadership.name: JD Vance
leadership.selection_detail: Ex officio role of the Vice President under Article I, Section 3 of the U.S. Constitution.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Populism & Working Class
top_issues.stance: Champions working-class concerns and criticizes corporate globalism in favor of nationalist economic policies.

top_issues.name: American Manufacturing
top_issues.stance: Supports reshoring manufacturing and protecting domestic industry from foreign competition.

top_issues.name: Senate Operations
top_issues.stance: Presides over Senate proceedings and breaks tie votes as Vice President.

experience.role: Vice President of the United States

leadership.name: Chuck Grassley
leadership.selection_detail: Senator since 1981; elected President pro tempore at the start of the 119th Congress. Traditionally held by the longest-serving member of the majority party.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Government Accountability
top_issues.stance: Focuses on congressional oversight and investigative authority to hold executive branch accountable.

top_issues.name: Agricultural Policy
top_issues.stance: Advocates for farm support programs and rural economic development benefiting Iowa agriculture.

top_issues.name: Fiscal Responsibility
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes budget control and scrutiny of government spending patterns.

leadership.name: John Thune
leadership.selection_detail: Senator since 2005. Elected Majority Leader by the Senate Republican Conference on Nov 13, 2024 to succeed Mitch McConnell.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Senate Leadership
top_issues.stance: Manages Senate Republican agenda, schedules legislation, and coordinates party strategy.

top_issues.name: Agricultural Policy
top_issues.stance: Supports farm programs and rural development initiatives benefiting South Dakota farmers.

top_issues.name: Fiscal & Trade Policy
top_issues.stance: Advocates for balanced budgets and favorable trade arrangements for American workers.

leadership.name: John Barrasso
leadership.selection_detail: Senator since 2007; elected Republican Whip by the caucus for the 119th Congress.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Energy Independence
top_issues.stance: Advocates for expanded oil, gas, and coal production from domestic sources.

top_issues.name: Environmental Policy
top_issues.stance: Questions climate regulations and supports energy development over environmental restrictions.

top_issues.name: Senate Operations
top_issues.stance: Enforces party discipline and manages Republican member voting on key legislation as Whip.

leadership.name: Chuck Schumer
leadership.selection_detail: Senator since 1999; has led Senate Democrats since 2017.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Social Programs
top_issues.stance: Advocates for protecting Social Security, Medicare, and other social safety net programs.

top_issues.name: Democracy & Voting Rights
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes voting rights protections and opposition to voter suppression efforts.

top_issues.name: Senate Operations
top_issues.stance: Leads Democratic caucus strategy and negotiations with Republican leadership on legislation.

leadership.name: Dick Durbin
leadership.selection_detail: Senator since 1997; has served as Democratic Whip since 2005.

contact.dc_office: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC 20510

top_issues.name: Immigration Reform
top_issues.stance: Advocates for comprehensive immigration reform including pathways to legal status for undocumented immigrants.

top_issues.name: Judicial Oversight
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes Senate Judiciary Committee role in confirming judges and scrutinizing executive actions.

top_issues.name: Senate Operations
top_issues.stance: Enforces Democratic party discipline and manages member support for legislation as Whip.

party_breakdown._note: Seat counts at the start of the 119th Congress on Jan 3, 2025; shifts with vacancies, special elections, and resignations.

leadership.name: Mike Johnson
leadership.selection_detail: Representative since 2017. Re-elected Speaker on Jan 3, 2025 on the first ballot with 218 votes. Speaker since Oct 2023.

top_issues.stance: Emphasizes budget control and reducing federal spending through committee oversight.

top_issues.name: House Operations

elections._note: Upcoming federal election cycles. State-specific ballot data lives in /data/{state}/elections.json.

upcoming.name: 2026 U.S. Midterm Elections
upcoming.description: All 435 seats in the U.S. House of Representatives and 33 of 100 U.S. Senate seats are up for regular election. Various special elections may also occur.

upcoming.name: 2028 U.S. Presidential Election
upcoming.description: Quadrennial presidential election. Incumbent Donald J. Trump is term-limited under the 22nd Amendment and may not seek a further term.
